input.background: Marketers use wording such as 'was $100, now $80' to indicate a price promotion. A study investigated whether between-store comparisons result in greater perceptions of value by consumers than within-store comparisons. Fifty consumers were randomly selected from a designated market area and randomly assigned to two groups: one group read a within-store price promotion advertisement ('was $100, now $80'), and the other group read a between-store price promotion advertisement ('$100 there, $80 here'). Consumers rated the value of the discount offer on a 10-point scale, and the value opinions of the two groups were compared.
input.question: Identify the data-collection method.

output.answer: Here, the experimental units are the consumers. Because the researchers controlled which price promotion ad—'within-store' or 'between-store'—the experimental units (consumers) were assigned to, a designed experiment was used to collect the data.

meta_info.caption: Representative Data- Price Promotion Study
meta_info.section: 1.6 Collecting Data: Sampling and Related Issues
